messages.content: Backdoor attacks are a type of poisoning attack where a trigger phrase is embedded in malicious documents. When the trigger appears in a query, the system returns attacker-controlled content.

Supervised learning is great for solving classification and regression problems!

Supervised learning is a type of machine learning where the algorithm learns from labeled training data. In this type of learning, each training example consists of an input and the desired output. The goal of supervised learning is to enable the algorithm to learn from the labeled data and make predictions on new, unseen data. Common algorithms used in supervised learning include Linear Regression, Decision Trees, Support Vector Machines, and Neural Networks. Applications of supervised learning include spam detection, image classification, and price prediction. The algorithm is trained on the labeled data to learn the relationship between the input and the desired output, and it can then use this learned relationship to make predictions on new data.
